news.headline: England's James and Rice to have fitness assessed
news.desc: England will assess the fitness of Reece James and Declan Rice before their final World Cup group game against Panama.

news.headline: Madibo given five-game ban for causing Kone leg break
news.desc: Qatar midfielder Assim Madibo is given a five-match ban for the challenge which resulted in a broken leg for Canada's Ismael Kone.

news.headline: England to face Scotland? World Cup knockout stage as it stands
news.desc: Use BBC Sport's projections tool with in-game updates to plot your country's route through the World Cup knockouts.

news.headline: Belgium going out? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - final group games
news.desc: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the final set of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

news.headline: Where to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
news.desc: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

news.headline: What are Scotland's chances of progressing as third-place finishers?
news.desc: With 32 of 48 teams advancing to the knockout rounds of the World Cup it is more difficult to be eliminated than to qualify.

news.headline: Tuchel's England are opposite to Southgate's - and built to beat top teams
news.desc: BBC Sport's football tactics writer Umir Irfan explains why Thomas Tuchel's England system is built to prosper against the world's top nations.

news.headline: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
news.desc: England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

news.headline: 'I'm back' - record-breaking Ronaldo answers critics
news.desc: Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo provides the perfect response to those that doubted him as he becomes the first player to score at six World Cups.

news.headline: Quiz: How well do you know birthday boy Messi at World Cups?
news.desc: Argentina great Lionel Messi turns 39 today and has been lighting up the World Cup this summer, but how well do you know his journey so far?

news.headline: As Messi turns 39, how he and Ronaldo keep breaking records
news.desc: Lionel Messi, 39, and Cristiano Ronaldo, 41, keep delivering on the world's biggest stage.

news.headline: Why Bellingham was not sent off for covering his mouth
news.desc: England midfielder Jude Bellingham was not shown a red card despite covering his mouth while talking to Ghana's Jordan Ayew.
